summaryTemplate: {{className}} is the {{patternRole}}. A non-virtual method fixes the algorithm's skeleton and calls virtual hooks that subclasses fill in, so the overall flow stays constant while steps vary. Pattern body lives at {{file}}:{{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}.
chosenRationaleTemplate: Tagged as Template Method because {{className}} pairs an orchestrator method (lines {{lineStart}}-{{lineEnd}}) with virtual hook methods it calls — the skeleton-plus-overridable-steps shape.

template_orchestrator_method.note: The algorithm skeleton. It is intentionally non-virtual so the sequence of steps is fixed; only the steps themselves vary.

template_virtual_hook.note: An overridable hook. Subclasses customise this step without being able to reorder or skip the skeleton.

runnerUpComparisonTemplates.Strategy: Strategy composes a varying algorithm as a separate object. Template Method keeps the algorithm in the base class and varies only named steps via inheritance — the variation point is finer-grained.
